rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography
Register match — Bootstrap voice trio
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 3-5 (Godin warmth), Lines 13-15 (Sutherland reframe), Lines 49-69 (pragmatic peer), Lines 111-125 (Sutherland comparison)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique
Training creator's name banned in student-facing body
Author-'we' ban — first-person-plural extension
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer terms (crux, compounding, memory, agents, judges, evals) contextualized on first use in body or embedded in structural sections

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector — cut reassurance prose
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 65-69: punchy question lands without over-softening; no carve-out needed (framing is operative, not slogan)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy, NOT in student-facing body
Watch out for planting jargon too early
rules_evaluated.evidence: memory (earned M1), context (earned M1), trigger (self-explanatory), agent (operational throughout), file paths (earned prework+M1-6)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: Verbs used: read, ask, draft, prepare, propose — all peer-flat

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 3-5 frame capability (you know agents can help), not deficit. Agency on student.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: memory, context, trigger, agent all contextualized; no cold technical terms

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately
No em-dashes; simple sentences
No narrating student's past or present state with patronizing drum-beat
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 11 (informing state, not narrating behavior); no gratuitous recaps

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename
rules_evaluated.evidence: No path-exposed links; reference line uses parenthetical (not a link)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
Canonical presentation shape
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 87-109: lead-in, canonical label, blank line, fenced block

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 90-109: no [BRACKETS]; open-hook embedded in Claude instruction (one-at-a-time questioning)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path
Markdown shout inside fenced blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: Fence uses bullets, no asterisk markup
